source.description: A six-panel pencil-and-colored-pencil process contact sheet reviewed as one cumulative drawing.
source.notes: One diagonal ear of corn on warm off-white paper. The cob, two peeled husks, kernel rows, silk tuft, color areas, and shadow are all established before the finishing panel.

process_strategy: The sequence fixes one diagonal cob-and-husk envelope, builds the peeled husks around the reserved cob, establishes the corn silhouette and kernel guides, adds the kernel rows and husk veins, then introduces restrained yellow and green pencil before final cleanup only.

final_elements.name: diagonal cob axis and outer envelope
final_elements.description: A light tapered oval construction for the corn cob with a wider leaf envelope around its lower half.

final_elements.name: two peeled husks, stem nub, and silk tuft
final_elements.description: Two overlapping pointed husk leaves peel away from the cob, with a short base stem and a small tuft of silk at the tip.

final_elements.name: rounded cob contour and curved kernel guides
final_elements.description: The exposed corn cob gets a clean rounded taper plus gently curved guide bands that follow its volume.

final_elements.name: individual kernel rows and husk veins
final_elements.description: Staggered rounded kernels fill the established cob grid while a few long veins describe the existing husks.

final_elements.name: yellow kernels, green husks, silk tone, and cast shadow
final_elements.description: Restrained golden-yellow pencil fills the kernels, leafy green tints the husks, pale tan warms the silk, and a soft gray shadow grounds the ear.

frames.visible_job: Places a light diagonal axis, tapered cob oval, and wider leaf envelope at one fixed scale.
introduces: diagonal cob axis and outer envelope

frames.visible_job: Builds two overlapping pointed husks around the reserved cob, then adds the base stem nub and tip silk.
introduces: two peeled husks, stem nub, and silk tuft
requires_prior_elements: diagonal cob axis and outer envelope

frames.visible_job: Clarifies the rounded cob taper inside the reserved opening and adds curved guide bands across it.
introduces: rounded cob contour and curved kernel guides
requires_prior_elements: diagonal cob axis and outer envelope
two peeled husks, stem nub, and silk tuft

frames.visible_job: Fills the existing cob guides with staggered rounded kernels and adds a few long veins to the established husks.
introduces: individual kernel rows and husk veins
requires_prior_elements: two peeled husks, stem nub, and silk tuft
rounded cob contour and curved kernel guides

frames.visible_job: Adds restrained yellow, green, tan, and gray pencil to the already drawn kernels, husks, silk, and ground area.
introduces: yellow kernels, green husks, silk tone, and cast shadow
requires_prior_elements: two peeled husks, stem nub, and silk tuft
rounded cob contour and curved kernel guides
individual kernel rows and husk veins

final_step.visible_job: Only strengthens established cob, husk, kernel, silk, color, and shadow marks without adding a new feature.

rejection_checks: No husk, stem, silk, cob contour, kernel row, vein, color area, or shadow may first appear in the final panel.
The ear must keep one diagonal pose, scale, crop, and husk arrangement in all six panels.
Kernel rows must follow the curved cob guides instead of becoming a flat checkerboard.
The finish must retain visible pencil texture and imperfect handmade joins rather than reading as vector corn clip art.
No plate, basket, face, lettering, decorative border, or extra produce may appear.
